a:r.a:t: Here is the system at a glance — four numbers that capture the scale. We have 13 specialized agents, each defined as a markdown file with YAML frontmatter rather than hard-coded classes. This "agents as data" approach means you can change an agent's behavior, tools, or mode by editing a markdown file and hitting the reload endpoint — no code deployment needed. 57 registered tools are organized into 9 functional groups: orchestration, RAG gateway, RAG workbench, analyst, graph gateway, memory, utility, discovery, and skills. Each agent gets a curated subset via its allowed_tools whitelist. 7 execution modes determine how each agent processes requests: basic, react, rag, planner, finalizer, verifier, memory_maintainer, and coordinator. The three-layer runtime separates concerns cleanly: RuntimeService handles orchestration and routing, RuntimeKernel manages session state and persistence, and QueryLoop runs the actual LLM calls. GraphRAG integration adds knowledge graph querying alongside traditional vector search. MCP enables dynamic tool discovery from external servers. And the worker orchestration system with 14 tools supports multi-agent coordination where a coordinator can decompose complex tasks across parallel workers. The 127-endpoint admin and control-panel API lets operators inspect and modify all of this at runtime.
